name: Example Steve
subtitle: Survivor of the Overworld - not real data
keyQuestion: What would I build if I knew the Ender Dragon couldn't touch me?
claudeRead: Steve is in a real transitional moment - the End portal is open, not theoretical. First solo mining session showed the deep caves produce a different kind of engagement than surface farming. Main risk: diamond scarcity shortens the search before the right gear lands. THE ENDER DRAGON is active but being noticed faster. Rest is the most significant unmet need. Beds explode in The End, so sleep stays unresolved.
progressionTimeline: Started the year punching trees in a bog biome with nothing to my name. Quit dirt huts cold turkey in January. Began clearing the inventory - and something else too. By March the End portal is located and the first enchanting table session has happened. The Ender Dragon is louder than ever, which means it's being threatened.

allies.name: Alex
allies.desc: Co-op partner since the tutorial world. Calls out creepers gently and shows up consistently.
allies.why: Knows the full inventory - the embarrassing wooden tools included.

allies.name: Wolf
allies.desc: Tamed with bones. Unconditional support. Not always the most talkative, but always in my corner.
allies.why: No performance required. Just sit. Good boy.

allies.name: Enchanting Session
allies.desc: 30 minutes at the enchanting table with lapis and a specific goal. Reliable focus ritual — the bookshelves help.
allies.why: Lowers the Ender Dragon's HP by at least a few points every time. Costs only time and lapis.

allies.name: Solo Cave Run
allies.desc: One hour underground, no build agenda, just explore. Phone on silent. Exits with more energy than it takes.
allies.why: Consistently resets the mental inventory. Works even when motivation is absent at spawn.

mainQuest.title: Defeat the Ender Dragon
mainQuest.description: Reach The End, destroy the crystals, and bring down the boss once and for all.
mainQuest.whyItMatters: More hours spent grinding than anywhere else. If the dragon keeps circling, everything else feels unfinished too.
mainQuest.doneWhen: Standing in The End with dragon egg in inventory and the credits actually rolling.

nextSteps.text: Locate a stronghold and find the End portal room
nextSteps.doneWhen: Eyes of Ender thrown, portal located - not just planned

nextSteps.text: Gear up: full diamond armour, enchanted bow, enough arrows
Spend two sessions this week mining the deep caves - no diamond requirement, just explore

sliderLabels.air: More aware than acting - noticing mob spawns without changing sleep habits yet
sliderLabels.fire: Motivation patchy - flickers of purpose but mostly running on torchlight
sliderLabels.water: Some aliveness - mining and cooking reliable, rest of biome still dark
sliderLabels.earth: Reasonably grounded - Creeper anxiety present but not dominating

limitingBeliefs.title: THE IMPOSTER
limitingBeliefs.belief: "I'm not skilled or geared enough to fight the bosses I actually want to fight."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Shows up when enchanting gear or entering new biomes. Reframes the learning curve as proof of being a noob forever.
limitingBeliefs.shortTermBenefit: Keeps ambition in the overworld and therefore safe from the humiliation of dying to a baby zombie.
limitingBeliefs.origin: Spawned in environments where being out-geared felt like the primary risk - performance over presence.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PERFECTIONIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "If I can't build the perfect base first, there's no point starting the main quest."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Main engine of procrastination. Creates an impossible aesthetic standard that ensures nothing is ever finished before the dragon is due.
limitingBeliefs.shortTermBenefit: The unbuilt mega-base can always be imagined as flawless. Completion would reveal the gaps.
limitingBeliefs.origin: Likely formed in creative mode where only exceptional builds were noticed - survival effort was invisible.

limitingBeliefs.title: THE PESSIMIST
limitingBeliefs.belief: "I always die before I reach the good loot."
limitingBeliefs.desc: Selective memory that catalogues every death screen and dismisses successful runs as luck or a seed exploit.
limitingBeliefs.shortTermBenefit: Low expectations mean a full chest feels like a win. Feels like protection against hype.
limitingBeliefs.origin: Unknown - possibly reinforced by a period where several hardcore worlds ended in close succession.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE PROBLEM SOLVER
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "I can figure out any redstone circuit if I stay in the room long enough."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Track record of working through ambiguous contraptions is real.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE LONG GAME
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "Slow progress toward diamonds beats fast progress toward gravel."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: Useful counter to urgency and biome-comparison.

empoweringBeliefs.title: THE BODY KNOWS
empoweringBeliefs.belief: "The aliveness I feel when mining the right cave is data, not coincidence."
empoweringBeliefs.desc: The qualitative difference between strip-mining and a deep-cave find is the clearest signal available.

skills.name: Set Boundaries
skills.description: Say no to side quests without over-explaining.
skills.whyItMatters: Without this, time and diamonds get consumed by everyone else's projects.

skills.name: Ask For Help
skills.description: Reach out to the server before getting lost in a cave.
skills.whyItMatters: How fast the main quest actually progresses.

skills.name: Stay Consistent
skills.description: Log in even when motivation is absent.
skills.whyItMatters: Mining, farming, enchanting all depend on this.

skills.name: Say No
skills.description: Decline build requests that conflict with the main quest.
skills.whyItMatters: Every yes to another player's mansion is a no to the End portal.

skills.name: Mining
skills.description: Navigate deep caves, read ore patterns, come back alive.
skills.whyItMatters: The capability that earns diamonds fastest.

skills.name: Crafting
skills.description: Build the right tools without wasting materials.
skills.whyItMatters: In resource-scarce play, crafting is the primary signal of competence.

skills.name: Exploring
skills.description: Read the terrain, find the structures, come back with a map.

sideQuests.title: Build a Proper Base
sideQuests.description: Upgrade from dirt hut to a real base with storage and beds.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: A respawn point is the foundation everything else is built on.
sideQuests.nextStep: Lay the foundation three sessions this week - no aesthetic requirement, just place blocks

sideQuests.title: Set Up an Enchanting Table
sideQuests.description: Build a full enchanting setup with bookshelves.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: The End fight needs enchanted gear, not just intent.
sideQuests.nextStep: Gather 15 bookshelves by end of March

sideQuests.title: Explore 12 Biomes This Year
sideQuests.description: One new biome per month, any type.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Deep exploration slows me down usefully and generates loot I couldn't find alone.
sideQuests.nextStep: Find a mushroom island before starting the next biome

sideQuests.title: Organise the Chest Room
sideQuests.description: Label and sort the entire storage system.
sideQuests.nextStep: Sort the overflow chests - the last pile has been sitting for a month

sideQuests.title: Learn Redstone
sideQuests.description: Build working automated farms over 12 months.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Automation opens up AFK time and is genuinely just for me.
sideQuests.nextStep: Complete one redstone tutorial every day for two weeks straight

completedQuests.title: Survive First Night
completedQuests.howResolved: Punched trees until I had a crafting table. Sealed in a dirt box and listened to mobs outside. Three months no-death run started there.

drains.name: Late-night inventory hoarding
drains.desc: Reliably ends with a full inventory and no idea what to do with it. Eats into sleep.

drains.name: Saying yes to co-op builds
drains.desc: Fills the session with other people's projects that don't align with the main quest.

drains.name: Skipping mining when the base hurts
drains.desc: The moment exploration would help most is exactly when it gets replaced by base tinkering.

flowSources.name: Mining
flowSources.desc: First 10 minutes are tense, then the cave opens up and the thinking quiets down.

flowSources.name: Cooking
flowSources.desc: Smelting and sorting - hands busy, mind relaxed. Reliably restorative.

flowSources.name: Music Discs
flowSources.desc: Stal playing in the jukebox alone - no audience, no performance.

flowSources.name: Village trading
flowSources.desc: Peaceful haggling where nothing critical is at stake.

flowSources.name: Building
flowSources.desc: Long creative sessions, late at night, no mobs on peaceful.

needs.name: Connection
needs.desc: A server with people who know the real build style. Good with a few, absent with most.

needs.name: Rest
needs.desc: Recovery time not immediately colonised by mob sounds or a to-do list. Significant deficit.

needs.name: Autonomy
needs.desc: Control over which biome to explore and when. Better than it was.

needs.name: Recognition
needs.desc: Feeling that the builds and runs are seen. The server is neutral at best.

values.name: Authenticity
values.desc: Playing in the style that actually feels right, not the meta loadout everyone else is running.

values.name: Family
values.desc: Being present and reliable for the wolves and villagers who matter most.

values.name: Health
values.desc: Full hearts and hunger bar as a non-negotiable foundation.

values.name: Growth
values.desc: Getting better at builds and biomes that matter.

values.name: Financial Security
values.desc: Enough emeralds to make genuine choices - freedom from villager price anxiety.

entries.title: Two Hours That Felt Like a Cave Find
events: Blocked out Saturday morning for deep cave exploration - phone in another room
Two uninterrupted hours in a massive cave system for the first time
insights: The two hours felt different - less like surface farming, more like contact with something real underground
PROCRASTINATION didn't fire once - a specific small task (find one diamond vein) made the difference
tensions: Whether the cave exploration is the actual direction or just a way of feeling productive without risking the End portal

entries.title: The Trade That Changed the Frame
events: Traded with a librarian villager for an Efficiency V book - she'd enchanted the same route two years ago
insights: SELF-DOUBT reframed the librarian's price as a test I was about to fail - I noticed it this time
tensions: Diamond scarcity is shortening the timeline in ways that might distort the gear decisions

entries.title: Three Months No-Death Run
events: Three months without dying on this world - marked it quietly, told Alex
insights: Staying alive required a quiet decision to stop fighting mobs I didn't need to fight
tensions: Rest is still a deficit - sessions feel productive but not restorative. Beds still explode in The End.

bosses.name: THE ENDER DRAGON
bosses.desc: The central force that frames every setback as evidence of fundamental under-gearing. Feels like a fair difficulty check. It isn't.
bosses.whyBoss: Every other enemy is this one in disguise - Procrastination is the Dragon saying you'll die, Self-Doubt is the Dragon questioning your enchants, the Comparison Trap is the Dragon finding external evidence you're a noob.
bosses.shortTermBenefit: Keeps standards high. Feels like appropriate caution before a high-stakes fight.
bosses.origin: Formed in environments where being under-geared was the primary risk - performance over presence.

vulnerabilities.title: Named in real time
vulnerabilities.desc: The Dragon loses power the moment it is caught mid-circle. Each live catch weakens its automaticity - calling it out loud is enough to create distance.

vulnerabilities.title: Evidence on the table
vulnerabilities.desc: The Dragon thrives on selective death screens. A written list of things that actually went well - not inspiration, just facts - starves it of its operating data.

vulnerabilities.title: Starting before ready
vulnerabilities.desc: The Dragon's main lever is the gap before entering the portal. Starting small - one Eye of Ender thrown, one block placed - collapses the gap before the Dragon can fill it.

subEnemies.name: PROCRASTINATION
subEnemies.desc: Masquerades as preparation. Rearranges the hotbar instead of entering the cave.

subEnemies.name: SELF-DOUBT
subEnemies.desc: Quiet voice that says other players have better gear. Speaks in first person so it sounds like truth.

subEnemies.name: COMPARISON TRAP
subEnemies.desc: Measures a messy mid-game against other players' fully-automated mega-bases.

currentEnemies.name: LATE-NIGHT INVENTORY HOARDING
currentEnemies.desc: Reliably ends with feeling worse than when it started. Fills the chest room and leaves the next session already in overflow.
currentEnemies.trigger: Evening wind-down session, especially after a difficult biome
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Decompression that asks nothing. Feels like progress without the vulnerability of actually using the materials.
currentEnemies.origin: Avoidance of the quiet - where unprocessed loot from the day tends to surface.

currentEnemies.name: OBLIGATION YES
currentEnemies.desc: Joining co-op builds to avoid the discomfort of declining. Fills the session with work that doesn't align with the main quest.
currentEnemies.trigger: Any server request that feels like it expects a yes
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Avoids conflict and the guilt of leaving someone underground alone. Feels like being a good teammate.
currentEnemies.origin: Unknown - likely formed where keeping allies comfortable felt safer than asserting the main quest.

currentEnemies.name: SKIP MINING WHEN STRESSED
currentEnemies.desc: Cutting the one session that would most help in the moments of highest Creeper anxiety. Always justified in the moment.
currentEnemies.trigger: High mob pressure, low torches, feeling behind on gear
currentEnemies.shortTermBenefit: Creates the illusion of more base time. Feels like a sensible trade-off.
currentEnemies.origin: The Ender Dragon framing exploration as optional when diamond counts are low.

graveyard.name: SUNDAY DREAD
graveyard.howDefeated: Lost its footing when the dirt hut was finally demolished. Without the spawn point it was protecting, it had nothing to attach to.

graveyard.name: THE HOARDER
graveyard.howDefeated: Named it properly for the first time. Hoarding disguised as being organised. Once it had a name it stopped working as cover.

graveyard.name: TWITTER SPIRAL
graveyard.howDefeated: Deleted the app after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse, every session.

activities.name: Cave Diving
activities.description: Focused time underground - mining, mob clearing, chest looting.
activities.whyItMatters: The diamonds I want won't mine themselves on the surface.

activities.name: Morning Patrol
activities.description: Perimeter check before 8am, clear all overnight spawns.
activities.whyItMatters: A clear base sets the tone for the whole session.

activities.name: Nether Runs
activities.description: Nether resource runs, three or four times a week.
activities.whyItMatters: Weekly consistency matters more than individual hauls.

achievements.title: No Death Run
achievements.description: Three months without a death screen on hardcore - the first time in many worlds.

achievements.title: The Organiser
achievements.description: Sorted 75% of the chest room in a single sustained session. Labels and everything.

achievements.title: Signal Over Noise
achievements.description: Deleted the Reddit app after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling like a noob.

classes.title: Builder
classes.description: Drawn to making structures. Finds meaning in the act of construction itself, not just the finished build.

classes.title: Explorer
classes.description: Biome-oriented and persistent when the biome actually matters. Learning to tell the difference between exploration and avoidance.

_xpLog.reason: Trade with librarian - genuine ask for help
Three months no-death run - No Death Run achievement
Skipped mining three times - named pattern, not bad luck
